blocks.en: The simplest way to go faster is to use more spectrum. Wi-Fi can bond several 20 MHz pieces into one wide channel: 20, 40, 80, 160, and — in Wi-Fi 7 — up to 320 MHz. Twice the width is roughly twice the data.
blocks.zh: 提速最简单的方式就是使用更多频谱。Wi-Fi 可以把若干个 20 MHz 片绑定成一个宽信道：20、40、80、160，以及在 Wi-Fi 7 中高达 320 MHz。带宽翻倍，数据大致翻倍。

blocks.en: Wider is not always better. A wide channel needs that much clean, free spectrum — harder to find on busy bands. That's why 320 MHz mostly lives in the roomy new 6 GHz band.
blocks.zh: 更宽并不总是更好。宽信道需要那么多干净、空闲的频谱——在繁忙频段更难找到。这也是 320 MHz 主要存在于宽敞的新 6 GHz 频段的原因。

blocks.en: Pick a width above and watch it grow as more 20 MHz sub-channels are bonded together. Each added block multiplies capacity — but also the chance that some part of it is occupied by a neighbour.
blocks.zh: 在上面选择一个带宽，看它随着更多 20 MHz 子信道被绑定而增长。每增加一块都成倍提升容量——但也增加了其中某部分被邻居占用的可能。

blocks.en: What happens when one sub-channel IS busy? Older Wi-Fi had to drop to a narrower channel. The next lesson and the Wi-Fi 7 track show a cleverer answer: puncture just the busy piece and keep the rest.
blocks.zh: 若其中一个子信道确实忙碌怎么办？旧版 Wi-Fi 只能退到更窄的信道。下一节与 Wi-Fi 7 轨道将展示更聪明的答案：只“打孔”掉忙碌的那一块，保留其余。

blocks.en: The operating channel width a STA uses is carried in fields such as the Channel Width field (9.4.1.21); bandwidth negotiation and dynamic bandwidth control reference it throughout the standard.
blocks.zh: STA 所用的工作信道带宽，由诸如 Channel Width 字段（9.4.1.21）等承载；带宽协商与动态带宽控制在标准中处处引用它。
